typecheck.summary: 0 errors — Next.js 15 async params fix resolved all prior typecheck failures

lint.summary: 3 warnings (no-img-element in ProductCard, BannerCarousel, product detail page) — non-blocking

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention for shared type definitions: 'Define shared TypeScript interfaces in the types/ directory. For Prisma model types used in client components, create a types/models.ts file rather than duplicating interfaces in each component.' Currently Product type is defined identically in 3 files.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: goods-home-page review found identical Product interface in app/page.tsx, components/goods/ProductCard.tsx, and components/goods/ProductGrid.tsx. This will worsen as more model types are needed for events, orders, etc.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Expand the Next.js 15 async params convention to cover client components: 'For client components receiving params as props, use useEffect to unwrap the Promise: useEffect(() => { params.then(p => setId(p.id)); }, [params]). Alternatively, use React.use(params) if the component can handle Suspense boundaries.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-nextjs15-async-params worker had to figure out the client component pattern independently. AGENTS.md only documents the server-side 'const { id } = await params' pattern. Both product-detail-page and fix reviews flagged this gap.

rejectedObservations.observation: Convention for vs next/image should be documented in AGENTS.md
rejectedObservations.reason: The use of plain tags is a pragmatic choice for external placeholder URLs (picsum.photos). Once real images are used, this convention may change. Documenting it now would prematurely codify a placeholder-era workaround. Lint warnings already flag this.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md should document which API routes require auth vs public
rejectedObservations.reason: The current middleware pattern protects all non-auth routes, which is the correct default. Documenting specific route auth scope is premature — when public endpoints are needed, the middleware matcher should be updated and documented at that time.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already has Next.js 15 async params convention
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate — already documented, confirmed by fix-nextjs15-async-params review
